summary: Split a large unsplit gm range by carving gm_17C0.c into smaller compilation units, adding new gm_1832.c and gm_18A5.c plus corresponding address-named headers. The PR also introduced gm_unsplit.h as a temporary umbrella include for address-named gm headers, reducing include churn while gm files continue to be split and renamed. Build configuration and GALE01 split/symbol metadata were updated for the new object boundaries. The decomp.dev report showed net matched-code progress of +2556 bytes and 24 new matches, mostly in gm_1832 and gm_18A5, while apparent broken matches/regressions were attributable to functions moving out of gm_17C0 into the new units.
what_changed: Updated GALE01 splits so melee/gm/gm_17C0.c now ends at 0x8018325C instead of spanning to 0x8019B8C4, and added new split entries for melee/gm/gm_1832.c from 0x8018325C to 0x8018A514 and melee/gm/gm_18A5.c from 0x8018A514 to 0x8019B8C4.
Added configure.py objects for melee/gm/gm_1832.c and melee/gm/gm_18A5.c as NonMatching units.

Moved hundreds of declarations out of gm_1601.h/gm_17C0.h into smaller address-range headers such as gm_16AE.h, gm_16F1.h, gm_17AD.h, gm_17BA.h, gm_1832.h, gm_18A5.h, gmresult.h, gmresultplayer.h, and gmregcommon.h.

Added src/melee/gm/gm_unsplit.h as an umbrella include for address-named unsplit gm headers including gm_1601.h, gm_16AE.h, gm_16F1.h, gm_17C0.h, gm_1832.h, gm_18A5.h, gm_19EF.h, gm_1A36.h, gm_1A3F.h, gm_1A45.h, gm_1AED.h, gm_1B03.h, and gm_1BFA.h.
Replaced many direct includes of gm_1601.h and several explicit gm header combinations across camera, debug, fighter, ground, item, library, menu, and gm files with gm/gm_unsplit.h.
Moved MajorSceneKind from gm/types.h into gm/forward.h, keeping it available with forward declarations rather than depending on the heavier gm/types.h.
Adjusted symbols.txt around split data boundaries, including refining lbl_803D9468 into smaller string symbols and merging lbl_804D6618/lbl_804D661C into an 0x8 sbss object.
smart_moves: The temporary gm_unsplit.h abstraction directly addresses the author-stated problem: address-named gm_1234.h-like headers can keep being split and renamed without forcing every consumer to be edited each time.
Kept new split units address-named, gm_1832 and gm_18A5, matching the existing provisional decomp convention until functionality is understood well enough for final names.

Made each gm source include its own header first, then use gm_unsplit.h for still-unsplit cross-file dependencies; this helps expose the intended public interface of each new slice while preserving build stability.
Updated splits.txt, configure.py, and symbols.txt together, which is essential when carving a new object range so build layout, decomp.dev accounting, and symbol metadata stay synchronized.
Moved MajorSceneKind into forward.h rather than leaving it in the broad types header, reducing dependency weight for declarations that need the enum.
decomp_lessons: When progressively splitting a large decompilation unit, add an explicit temporary umbrella header with comments documenting that it is only for still-unsplit, unstable address-named files.

Do not force every downstream file to chase provisional gm_1234.h names during active file splitting; centralize those temporary includes and replace them with proper named headers once functions settle.
Address-range headers are useful intermediate artifacts: keep declarations near the new object range and include forward headers for only the types needed.

A split can look like broken matches in the old unit while still being net progress if functions moved into new units; read decomp.dev reports in terms of total project bytes and new object ownership.
Moving enums or shared declarations from a heavy types header into forward.h can reduce include coupling during header cleanup.
naming_conventions: Temporary unsplit gm files and headers use address-range names such as gm_1601, gm_16AE, gm_16F1, gm_17C0, gm_1832, and gm_18A5.
The umbrella include is named gm_unsplit.h and its file comment says it should be used only for functions from unsplit gm files whose names are in flux.
Function declarations retain address-comment prefixes like /* 18325C */ and provisional names like fn_8018325C until semantic names are known.
Lifecycle callbacks keep suffixes such as _OnEnter and _OnFrame, as seen in symbols reported by decomp.dev including gm_801877A8_OnEnter and gm_8018776C_OnFrame.
Major scene enum entries use the MJ_ prefix, for example MJ_TITLE, MJ_MENU, MJ_VS, MJ_TOURNAMENT, MJ_BOOT, and MJ_COUNT.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Carved gm_17C0.c at exact text/data/bss/sdata/sbss/sdata2 boundaries into gm_17C0.c, gm_1832.c, and gm_18A5.c, then reflected those boundaries in config/GALE01/splits.txt.
Added new nonmatching objects to configure.py so the build system recognizes the split units even before all functions match.
Preserved and relocated static data declarations into the new units, such as lbl_804D4190 and gm_804771C4 in gm_18A5.c, to keep data ownership aligned with the new object boundaries.
Updated symbols.txt for object/string sizes near the moved data, avoiding stale symbol spans after the split.
Used header splitting and include replacement as a matching-enabler rather than only a cosmetic cleanup; decomp.dev reported 24 new matched functions and +2556 bytes matched code.

review_feedback.feedback: Author highlighted gm_unsplit.h as the most notable change, intended to prevent repeated include edits while gm_1234.h-like headers continue to be renamed and split.
GALE01 report showed matched code 35.46%, +0.07%, +2556 bytes, with 24 new matches mostly in main/melee/gm/gm_1832 and main/melee/gm/gm_18A5.

The bot listed 7 broken matches and many regressions in gm_17C0, but the affected functions correspond to code moved into the new gm_1832/gm_18A5 units; no human review comments were present in the slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename gm_1832.h or gm_18A5.h to semantic game-mode filenames.
Search for remaining includes of gm_unsplit.h to identify consumers that can be migrated to proper split headers.
Audit gm/types.h and gm/forward.h after this PR to ensure only lightweight shared declarations remain in forward.h.
Compare decomp.dev reports after subsequent gm splits to distinguish true regressions from moved-function accounting.
